geosR 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation

    geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation

    geosR_tutorial.Rmd

    Introduction

    The geosR package provides a generalized geostatistical workflow to calculate spatial resources (volume, metal/content tonnage, and average grades). It simplifies the typical gstat + sf pipeline utilized by spatial geologists.

    There are 5 core functions: 1. fit_var(): Fit a variogram model to spatial drillhole data. 2. est_krige(): Run an ordinary kriging block model prediction. 3. calc_res(): Intersect block model results with an area of interest polygon to compute volume, grades, and tonnage. 4. ev_rest(): Perform resource reconciliation by comparing calculated totals against actual production realization. 5. plot_res(): Visually plot the spatial resource distributions across your blocks.

    library(geosR)
library(sf)
#> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
library(terra)
#> terra 1.8.93

    Basic Workflow Example

    Below is a theoretical workflow implementing these functions.

    1. Load Data

    Assume you have loaded a spatial sf object drill_data and an area boundary polygon.

    # drill_data <- st_as_sf(df, coords=c("X","Y"), crs=32748)

    2. Variogram Modeling

    Fit a spherical model to your spatial grade data:

    var_model <- fit_var(
  data = drill_data, 
  formula = grade ~ 1, 
  cutoff = 250, 
  width = 50, 
  model_type = "Sph",
  sill = 0.02, 
  range = 150
)

# Plot the model
plot(var_model$variogram, var_model$model, main="Spherical Model")

    3. Ordinary Kriging

    Generate a calculation grid, and predict the variable utilizing the fitted model.

    calc_grid <- st_make_grid(st_bbox(area), cellsize = 20)

kriged_grade <- est_krige(
  data = drill_data, 
  formula = grade ~ 1,
  grid = calc_grid, 
  vgm_model = var_model$model
)

    4. Calculate Resources

    Compute your overall volume and tonnage inside an area parameter.

    # Convert kriged points to rasters for both grade and thickness
raster_grade <- as(st_rasterize(kriged_grade["var1.pred"]), "SpatRaster")
raster_thick <- as(st_rasterize(kriged_thick["var1.pred"]), "SpatRaster")

# Density determines your resulting tonnage unit (assuming grade is raw % or kg/m3)
my_resources <- calc_res(raster_grade, raster_thick, area, density = 1.0)

# Check calculated figures per Area Polygon
print(my_resources$table)

    5. Evaluate Realization

    Determine a recovery factor by contrasting with actual plant performance.

    final_table <- ev_rest(my_resources$table, actual_production = 83000)
print(final_table)

    6. Plot the Estimate

    Generate standard plots to add to reports.

    plot_res(
  tonnage_raster = my_resources$raster, 
  area = area, 
  title = "Resource Estimate Block A"
)

    Developed by Ghozian Karami.
